lede: A look at July 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Polina Bogusevich and Vladimir Boisa, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: July 2025 Central Texas floods
events.desc: A devastating flood strikes the Texas Hill Country, killing at least 108 people.

events.title: 1982 kidnapping of Iranian diplomats
events.desc: Three Iranian diplomats and a journalist are kidnapped in Lebanon by Phalange forces, and their fate remains unknown.

events.title: Israel Defense Forces
events.desc: Israeli commandos raid Entebbe airport in Uganda, rescuing all but four of the passengers and crew of an Air France jetliner seized by Palestinian terrorists.

events.title: William Shockley
events.desc: William Shockley announces the invention of the junction transistor.

events.title: Philippines
events.desc: After 381 years of near-continuous colonial rule by various powers, the Philippines attains full independence from the United States.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Hamel, a successful attack by the Australian Corps against German positions near the town of Le Hamel on the Western Front.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Siege of Vicksburg: The Confederate army in Vicksburg, Mississippi surrenders to Union forces under Ulysses S. Grant after 47 days of siege, contributing to the Union capture of the Mississippi River.

events.title: Grand Junction Railway
events.desc: Grand Junction Railway, the world's first long-distance railway, opens between Birmingham and Liverpool.

events.title: Theodosius II
events.desc: Emperor Theodosius II, age 13, yields power to his older sister Aelia Pulcheria, who reigned as regent and proclaimed herself empress (Augusta) of the Eastern Roman Empire.

deaths.text: Lyndon Byers, Canadian ice hockey player and radio host
Bob Ross, American painter and television host
Władysław Sikorski, Polish general and politician, 9th Prime Minister of the Second Republic of Poland

observances.text: Christian feast day: Saint Pier Giorgio Frassati
Christian feast day: Elizabeth of Aragon (or of Portugal)
